description: beyond-1B.3b Task 3: source-adjudicated overlay for the first 42 `known-genuine-divergences.json` sites (the `builtin-catalog-fp-collision` shape). Every entry's `receiver_kind`/`catalog_key`/`callee_text`/`arity` was derived INDEPENDENTLY of the fresh resolver's output: by opening the CDO_WS source at (unit, line), reading the actual call syntax and receiver type, confirming `catalog_key` is a real member of the structural builtin catalog (`al_call_hierarchy::program::resolve::builtins::is_global_builtin` for `receiver_kind=Global`, `al_call_hierarchy::program::resolve::member_catalog::member_builtin` for `PageInstance`/`Record`/`RecordRef`/`Framework`), and confirming NO competing same-name `procedure` is declared in the same unit (Task-1 lookup-precedence shadow check, grep-verified). `catalog_key` is a CANONICAL CATALOG STRING in the exact `BuiltinId` format the catalog itself produces (bare lowercase name for global builtins, `Prefix::method` for member builtins) — never a serialized fresh `Edge`/`CanonicalTarget`/graph-node id. `source_sha256` is the SHA-256 of the full CDO_WS unit file content at adjudication time (2026-07-01 for the first 44 entries, 2026-07-01 for the 7 `follow-up plan v2.1 Task 3` entries below, 2026-07-03 for entry 52); `tests/program_resolve_harness.rs::cdo_genuine_wrong_is_precedence_adjudicated` re-hashes the CURRENT unit at test time and FAILS on any mismatch (source drift), then independently RE-DERIVES the verdict from live source + the catalog functions (never from this file's own `verdict`/`catalog_key` fields, and never by calling `resolve_full_program` or reading a fresh-computed edge) and asserts it matches. Entries 43-44 (beyond-1B.3b Task 5.5, `receiver_kind: CrossAppSourceProcedure`) use a DIFFERENT, second target shape: `catalog_key` is empty; `target_kind`/`target_app_guid`/`target_object_lc`/`target_routine_lc` instead identify a REAL procedure independently verified against Microsoft Base Application's own embedded (ShowMyCode) source — re-extracted and grep-checked directly from the real `.app`, never from a fresh-computed edge. Entries 45-51 (follow-up plan v2.1 Task 3, `resolve_bare` Step 3 — bare implicit-Rec dispatch) reuse the SAME `CrossAppSourceProcedure` target shape but with a BARE `callee_text` (`"Navigate"`, no receiver) instead of a qualified member call — `verify_cross_app_source_procedure_override` (`tests/program_resolve_harness.rs`) accepts both caller-side shapes for this receiver_kind (extended when these 7 were added): each is a distinct Page whose `SourceTable` is a different Base App posted-document-header table, all newly resolved once `resolve_bare`'s Step 3 (bare unqualified calls implicitly dispatching to `Rec`) was implemented; every target table's `procedure Navigate()` was independently re-extracted and read from the real Base App `.app`'s embedded ShowMyCode source. Entry 52 (record-field chains plan Task 4, `receiver_kind: Framework` — NEW receiver kind added when `infer_receiver_type`'s Step 3a, bare implicit-Rec QUOTED-field receivers, was implemented): `catalog_key`'s PREFIX (before `::`) is a `classify_type_text`-recognized Framework type name (`Text`); `derive_verdict` resolves it via `classify_type_text(prefix)` -> `ParsedType::Framework(kind)` -> `member_builtin(MemberCatalogKind::Framework(&kind), method)`, reusing the SAME classification function the resolver itself uses (never re-deriving type parsing ad hoc) — the receiver itself is a bare QUOTED table field (`"Azure Blob Private Endpoint URL"`, Text[250], source-verified) rather than a fixed keyword receiver like `CurrPage`/`Rec`, so `assert_shape_matches_receiver_kind` does not apply a PageInstance-style fixed-token check to it. `run_cdo_semantic_audit` (`semantic_golden.rs`) applies every `l3_error_intrinsic` entry here as an IN-MEMORY override of the frozen `cdo-anon.json` L3 golden's target for that site before diffing fresh (building either target shape per `apply_adjudicated_overrides`, which is receiver-kind-agnostic and required NO changes for the new `Framework` kind) — `cdo-anon.json` itself is never mutated. Entries 53-54 (argtype-dispatch-and-page-catalog plan, Task 1, `receiver_kind: PageInstanceVar` — NEW receiver kind added when the Page/Report instance-catalog completion made `SetTableView`/`SetRecord`/`GetRecord`/`SetSelectionFilter` resolve on GENERAL declared Page-typed variables, not just the `CurrPage`/`Page` singleton): `catalog_key` uses the exact same `PageInstance::<method>` catalog string as `receiver_kind: PageInstance`, since PAGE_INSTANCE is ONE shared platform-intrinsic catalog regardless of receiver origin (`derive_verdict` delegates both kinds to the identical `member_builtin` check); the two kinds differ only in what `assert_shape_matches_receiver_kind` independently verifies about the receiver TOKEN — `PageInstance` requires the fixed `CurrPage`/`Page` keyword, `PageInstanceVar` instead requires (`unit_declares_page_typed_var`) an independently-confirmed `<receiver>: Page ...` variable declaration elsewhere in the SAME unit, and explicitly REJECTS the `CurrPage`/`Page` keyword (that shape must use `PageInstance`). Both entries' root cause is a duplicate-trigger-name variant of the `builtin-catalog-fp-collision` class: the flagged object declares TWO field controls with their own same-named `trigger OnAssistEdit()`, and L3's frozen golden site key (which carries no per-control qualifier) mis-pairs the site with the OTHER trigger's unrelated call. All 54 entries adjudicated `l3_error_intrinsic` (fresh is correct; L3 has a genuine golden-side defect — a mis-resolved platform intrinsic for entries 1-42 and 52-54, a mis-assigned/collapsed cross-app site for entries 43-44, and an unmodeled bare-implicit-Rec dispatch shape for entries 45-51, since L3's frozen golden predates that resolver capability). Zero `fresh_false_builtin`, zero `needs_manual_review`.

entries.note: ExportTemplateAction.OnAction: `CurrPage.SetSelectionFilter(EMailTemplateHeader)`. CurrPage is this page's own instance; SetSelectionFilter is a PAGE_INSTANCE catalog member. No `procedure SetSelectionFilter(` declared in this unit.
OnValidate: `if Evaluate(i, TableNoName) or (TableNoName = '') then`. Bare unqualified global call; `evaluate` is a global-builtin catalog member. No `procedure Evaluate(` declared in this unit.
Linked to Field No. OnLookup: `CurrPage.Update();` after a successful lookup dialog. No `procedure Update(` declared in this unit.
From E-Mail OnValidate: `CurrPage.Update(true);`. Same unit/catalog reasoning as line 368.
PDF Sign Certificate OnValidate: `Error(ErrorPdfPasswordAndCertificate);`. Bare global call; `error` is a global-builtin catalog member. No `procedure Error(` declared in this unit.

Print action OnAction: `Cust.SetRecFilter();` where `Cust: Record Customer` is a local var. Record is a sealed platform type — no user procedure can shadow `SetRecFilter`; confirmed no `procedure SetRecFilter(` in this unit regardless.
Print action OnAction: `Cust.SetRecFilter();`, same shape as Page 6175280.
TryConnect success OnAction: `Message(ConnectionSuccessText);`. Bare global call; no `procedure Message(` declared in this unit.
Office 365 Setup OnAction: `if Confirm(Text001, false, Code, 'Office 365') then`. Bare global call; no `procedure Confirm(` declared in this unit.

CORRECTED (pageext-merge-and-final-residual plan, Task 2, dated 2026-07-04) from the stale builtin-catalog-fp-collision adjudication ("run is a global-builtin catalog member ... NO local procedure Run( anywhere in this 141-line page object"): that check only inspected the SAME UNIT (the calling page file) for a competing procedure, never the page's own SourceTable (a DIFFERENT file/object). RunAction.OnAction: bare `Run();` (legacy C/SIDE-form global invocation — this action has no `RunObject` property, unlike the sibling JobQueueEntries action). Task 2 grounded `Run` (MS Learn: page-run--method / codeunit-run-method) as having NO bare-call form anywhere in AL — always receiver-qualified (`MyCodeunit.Run()`, `Page.RunModal(...)`) — so the PROBE-THEN-DECIDE collision guard no longer treats it as a competing global/page-instance reading. The page's `SourceTable = "CDO E-Mail Job"` (Table 6175280) declares its OWN `procedure Run()` (`.dependencies/CDO/Table/CDOEMailJob.Table.al:192`, independently re-verified directly in the live CDO_WS source — same app as the caller, never a compiled .alpackages dependency) — fresh now correctly resolves the bare call there, WITH SOURCE EVIDENCE, via resolve_bare's Step 3 implicit-Rec dispatch. L3's frozen golden (minted before bare-implicit-Rec dispatch existed) still pairs this site/callee_fp with the stale Builtin(run) reading, which is now itself the error. CORRECTED AGAIN (Task 2 REVIEW fix, dated 2026-07-04): the preceding sentence is FALSE. An independent HMAC-based re-verification of the RAW, un-overridden L3 golden (cdo-anon.json) for this exact site established that its recorded target has ALWAYS been Table 6175280 "CDO E-Mail Job".Run — IDENTICAL to the target_* fields above. L3 was never wrong here; the actual (now-closed) defect was fresh's OWN pre-Task-2 answer (Catalog/Builtin(run)), which Task 2 fixed for an unrelated reason (the INSTANCE_ONLY_NEVER_BARE grounding). This override is consequently a documented NO-OP: it writes back the exact target the raw golden already supplies, and removing it would not change run_cdo_semantic_audit's outcome. Retained (not deleted) as the historical adjudication record — see known-genuine-divergences.json's description for the full rationale.

L3's frozen golden pairs this callee_fp with `CheckAzureContainerPerCompany` (called from a DIFFERENT field's OnValidate trigger at lines 300/324) — the same L3 line/routine-key misattribution already documented for the CopyStr/MaxStrLen entries on this line.

beyond-1B.3b Task 5.5 (newly reachable once Base App is wired into the closure): SendCustomAction.OnAction: `SalesInvHeader.SendRecords();` where `SalesInvHeader: Record "Sales Invoice Header"`. `SendRecords` is a REAL, ordinary `procedure SendRecords()` declared on Microsoft Base Application's Table 112 "Sales Invoice Header" (`src/Sales/History/SalesInvoiceHeader.Table.al` inside the real Base App .app's embedded ShowMyCode source, independently re-extracted and read) — its body internally raises the nested `OnBeforeSendRecords` integration event, which several Continia CDO codeunit procedures subscribe to. L3's frozen golden pairs this exact site/callee_fp with those 5 downstream Continia subscriber procedures instead of the directly-named `SendRecords` procedure itself — an L3 modeling/bookkeeping defect (it collapsed the call chain past the actual callee), not a fresh bug: the call literally, unambiguously names `SendRecords`, which fresh correctly resolves with Evidence::Source to its real declaration.

beyond-1B.3b Task 5.5 (newly reachable once Base App is wired into the closure): Enabled.OnValidate: `Rec.Enabled := CustomerConsentMgt.ConfirmUserConsent();` where `CustomerConsentMgt: Codeunit "Customer Consent Mgt."`. `ConfirmUserConsent` is a REAL, ordinary `procedure ConfirmUserConsent(): Boolean` declared on Microsoft Base Application's Codeunit 1820 "Customer Consent Mgt." (`src/CustomerConsentMgt.Codeunit.al` inside the real Base App .app's embedded ShowMyCode source, independently re-extracted and read) — a simple UI-dialog confirmation, no events involved. L3's frozen golden pairs this exact site/callee_fp with `GetESealService` on the SAME table's OTHER field-OnValidate trigger (a completely unrelated call at a different source line, `ESealMgt.GetESealService()`) — a clear L3 site/line-bookkeeping defect, not a fresh bug: fresh correctly resolves the literal callee `CustomerConsentMgt.ConfirmUserConsent` with Evidence::Source to its real declaration.

argtype-dispatch-and-page-catalog plan Task 1: the "From E-Mail Address Field" control's own `trigger OnAssistEdit()`: `Field.SetRange(TableNo, "First Table in Report"); FieldsWithRelationPage.SetTableView(Field);` - `FieldsWithRelationPage: Page "CDO Fields (With Relation)"` is a local variable; SetTableView is a PAGE_INSTANCE catalog member (no `procedure SetTableView(` declared in this unit). This page ALSO declares a SECOND, unrelated `trigger OnAssistEdit()` on the earlier "Report Selection Usage" field control (`ReportSelectionUsageLookup(); ReportSelectionUsage := Format(...); SetEditableFields();`) - L3's frozen golden's (unit,line,callee_fp) key does not disambiguate same-named triggers on different field controls and mis-pairs THIS site with the OTHER trigger's `ReportSelectionUsageLookup` target instead - the same duplicate-trigger-name variant of the builtin-catalog-fp-collision class documented above (entries 3-4/39-42/52), newly exposed because fresh previously emitted no target at all for a general Page-variable SetTableView call.

This page ALSO declares a SECOND, unrelated `trigger OnAssistEdit()` on the earlier "Description" field control (`LineAssistEdit();`, a local page procedure) - L3's frozen golden's (unit,line,callee_fp) key does not disambiguate same-named triggers on different field controls and mis-pairs THIS site with the OTHER trigger's `LineAssistEdit` target instead - the same duplicate-trigger-name variant of the builtin-catalog-fp-collision class documented above (entries 3-4/39-42/52), newly exposed because fresh previously emitted no target at all for a general Page-variable SetTableView call.
